technical.lead: ホワイトハウスがフロンティアモデルのリリース前審査EOを検討
technical.body: The New York Times reports that the Trump administration is weighing an executive order creating a federal working group of tech executives + government officials to design a pre-release vetting process for new AI models, with senior administration officials briefing Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI on the plans last week. The immediate catalyst is Anthropic's Claude Mythos — announced Apr 7, with the model finding tens of thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities including a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug — and Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei publicly framing a cyber "moment of danger." Pairs with the May 1 UK AI Safety Institute cyber evaluation that placed GPT-5.5 at 71.4% and Mythos at 68.6% on Expert-tier offensive-cyber tasks (Opus 4.7 at 48.6%), and that recorded GPT-5.5 + Mythos completing AISI's 32-step "The Last Ones" corporate-network attack range end-to-end in 2 of 10 and 3 of 10 attempts respectively (an AISI-estimated 20-human-hour exploit chain). The UK NHS has already ordered tech leaders to wall off hundreds of open-source GitHub repositories with a May deadline citing Mythos exposure. The proposal would mark a stark reversal of the Jan 2025 Trump rescission of the Biden AI EO and would land the first credible federal AI pre-release-review framework under the current administration.

citations.label: PYMNTS - In an About-Face, Trump Administration Considers Vetting New AI Models
Free Malaysia Today - White House weighs vetting AI models before public release
AISI - Our evaluation of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 cyber capabilities
The Decoder - GPT-5.5 matches Claude Mythos in cyber attack tests

technical.lead: Cerebrasが$26.6Bターゲット評価でIPOを発出
technical.body: Cerebras Systems announces the launch of its initial public offering, pricing 28M shares at $115-$125 each to raise approximately $3.5B at a $26.6B market cap at the high end — would be the largest tech IPO of 2026 if priced at the high end and would clear its Feb 2026 Series H investors at $23B. The S-1 disclosed $510M trailing revenue and a multi-year warrant + loan structure with OpenAI worth more than $10B; in Dec 2025 OpenAI loaned Cerebras $1B secured by warrants for over 33M shares (~12% of the float). The angel investor roster includes Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever, and Adam D'Angelo. The deal is the first public-market test of an AI-accelerator startup with deep frontier-lab supply contracts, and sets the IPO-shape template for SambaNova, Groq, Tenstorrent, and the second-tier inference-chip cohort.

citations.label: BEAMSTART - Cerebras Gears Up for $26 Billion IPO Fueled by OpenAI's $10B Deal

technical.lead: n8n CVE-2026-21858 critical RCE (CVSS 10.0) + AI-workflow CVEウェーブ
technical.body: Multiple critical CVEs land on the no-code AI-workflow harness category in the same window. n8n CVE-2026-21858 (CVSS 10.0, codenamed Ni8mare by Cyera Research Labs) is an unauthenticated remote-code-execution flaw caused by content-type confusion in webhook request handling — attackers forge uploaded files, read arbitrary local files, forge administrator sessions, and execute commands on the underlying host; affected versions are 1.65.0 and earlier through pre-1.121.0; patched in 1.121.0; downstream prompt-injection scenarios let an attacker leak read-out files via the n8n LLM-chatbot node. Flowise CVE-2026-41264 is a prompt-injection-driven RCE on the Flowise CSV Agent node that lets a chatflow user coax the LLM into emitting attacker-controlled Python that the Flowise server executes. LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 SSRF (published Apr 21) saw the first exploitation attempts against Sysdig Threat Research honeypots within 12 hours 31 minutes. Georgia Tech's Vibe Security Radar attributed 35 CVEs in March 2026 directly to AI coding tools, with researchers estimating the true count is 5-10x higher across the open-source ecosystem.

citations.label: Aikido - n8n Critical Vulnerability CVE-2026-21858 Unauthenticated RCE Explained

technical.lead: Mistral AI Workflows public preview: Temporal-powered enterprise orchestration
technical.body: Mistral AI introduces Workflows, a Temporal-powered orchestration engine for enterprise multi-step AI processes, now in public preview as part of the Mistral Studio platform. Developers define workflows in Python combining models + agents + external connectors with durability, observability, and fault tolerance; the architecture separates control and data planes — orchestration runs on Mistral-managed infrastructure while execution workers and data processing remain inside the customer environment (cloud, on-prem, or hybrid). Already running in production at ASML, ABANCA, CMA-CGM, France Travail, La Banque Postale, and Moeve, the Workflows release is the middle layer of Mistral's three-part enterprise stack: Forge (custom-model training, March 2026 launch at NVIDIA GTC) at the bottom, Workflows in the middle, and Vibe (coding-agent platform) at the top. Pairs with the May 5 Anthropic 10-agent finance launch as the EU-frontier-lab counter-shape to the US frontier-lab vertical-agent push and lands the first Temporal-backed orchestration engine purpose-built for LLMs in enterprise production.

citations.label: Mistral AI - Workflows for work that runs the business

technical.lead: AISI cyber eval: GPT-5.5 + Mythosが32-step Last Onesアタック・レンジを通過
technical.body: The UK AI Safety Institute publishes its May 1 cyber capability evaluations placing GPT-5.5 at 71.4% on Expert-tier offensive-cyber tasks (matching Anthropic's Mythos at 68.6%, with Opus 4.7 at 48.6%). AISI's headline benchmark, "The Last Ones" (TLO), is a 32-step corporate-network attack simulation spanning initial reconnaissance through full network takeover, which AISI estimates would require humans 20 hours to complete. GPT-5.5 completed the chain end-to-end in 2 of 10 attempts (the second model ever to do so), following Mythos at 3 of 10 (the first). The evaluations land alongside Anthropic's Project Glasswing — which routes Mythos access exclusively to vetted security partners working on local vulnerability detection, black-box binary testing, endpoint security, and penetration testing — and frame the operational backdrop for the May 4-5 White House pre-release-vetting EO trail.

citations.label: AISI - Our evaluation of Claude Mythos Preview's cyber capabilities
